spec_match.rationale: The cleanest demonstration of the spec change in the feature: a single :root ::selection rule driven by --sel-bg / --sel-fg custom properties propagates through the highlight chain to nested <code>, <strong>, <em>, blockquote-like .quote elements, and three explicit nesting levels.

interactive.rationale: Five theme buttons (default/blue/emerald/rose/invert) drive a single class swap on <body>; the ::selection cascade then updates everywhere. A live selection indicator surfaces what the user just selected — feedback loop is immediate.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a theme switch with a single root rule reaching every nesting level; page delivers a three-level nesting demo + an article body + a before/after explanation.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the 'pre-134 had to repeat for every selector' case in the explanation, the invert theme which flips bg/fg, and the selection-indicator gracefully handles 'no selection'.

references.rationale: see-also lists four sibling concepts only. Missing https://chromestatus.com/feature/5090853643354112 (invariant #3) and the css-pseudo-4 highlight-cascade spec link.

design_system.rationale: Theme classes use design-token colours correctly. BUT .compare-cell-head.before uses raw hex `#ffeef0`/`#991b1b` and .after uses `#e6f4ea`/`#065f46` — raw hex violates invariant #5.

openQuestions.title: Add the see-also references
openQuestions.detail: see-also is missing the chromestatus link (https://chromestatus.com/feature/5090853643354112, invariant #3) and the css-pseudo-4 highlight-cascade spec URL.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex compare-cell-head colours with design tokens
openQuestions.detail: The before/after compare cells use raw hex which breaks invariant #5 (CSS variables only, never raw hex). Switch to color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-rose) 15%, transparent) for the tint and var(--accent-rose) / var(--accent-emerald) for the foreground.

openQuestions.title: Probe is structurally wrong
openQuestions.detail: Feature detection uses CSS.supports('selector(::selection)') which returns true on every modern engine regardless of whether highlight inheritance is wired. The probe will lie on Firefox/Safari. Replace with behavioural detection or remove.

openQuestions.title: Show the rule-count delta
openQuestions.detail: The headline is 'one rule for the whole page'. A 'lines of CSS needed' counter (post: 1, pre: N for each nesting level) would make the win quantitative.

summary: Best concept in the feature for theming — five themes, single rule, three nesting levels, live selection indicator. Three fixes: chromestatus link, raw hex → design tokens, fix the bogus support probe.
